title: QUA-671 cleanup — stop wait_guard polling, freeze WAIT_GUARD writes until OWNER signoff
description:
## Context

Parent issue QUA-671 (P0-13 T6 deploy manifest dry-run) just flipped to `blocked` (DL-046 enforcement, durable record on the parent). Concurrently, the audit (Board Advisor 2026-05-09) found `scripts/ops/qua671_wait_guard.ps1` running in 6 worktrees simultaneously and producing 2,069 WAIT_GUARD-JSON files in `docs/ops/`.

Until OWNER signs off the manifest, no new WAIT_GUARD files should be written. The comment thread on QUA-671 is the durable record.

## Action

1. Locate every running instance of `scripts/ops/qua671_wait_guard.ps1` across the `agents/*` and other worktrees. Stop all but one (keep at most one canonical instance, ideally none).
2. Confirm via process listing that no new `qua671_wait_guard.ps1` invocation is fired by any cron/task/routine.
3. Add a guard at the top of the script: if `QUA-671.status == 'blocked'`, exit 0 with a single log line (no JSON write). This makes the script DL-046-compliant if it is reactivated later.
4. Sweep `docs/ops/QUA-671_WAIT_GUARD_*.json` from before the cutoff into `docs/ops/QUA-archived/QUA-671/` to keep the active ops dir clean. Do NOT delete (forensic value retained).

## Acceptance

- `Get-Process | Where-Object {$_.CommandLine -match 'qua671_wait_guard'}` returns at most one row.
- No new `docs/ops/QUA-671_WAIT_GUARD_*.json` file with mtime after the cutoff.
- Script has the `status==blocked → no-op` guard committed.

## References

- Parent: QUA-1024 (Board Advisor audit, P1)
- Related: QUA-671 (the issue being polled)
- DL-046 (status, not comments, drives the briefing)
- Memory `feedback_blocked_reason_schema_gap`: blockedReason persistence is partial, comment thread is the durable record.
